build_check.description: The project launches with the headless command, exits with code 0, and produces no fatal errors. Returns 0 or 1; the rest of the score is multiplied by this gate.

requirements.description: Story advances as paced dialogue/narration the player drives forward (clicking or a key reveals the next line) over recognizable journey and spirit encounter scenes, with a readable speaker, rather than dumping all text at once or auto-scrolling. Strong submissions integrate dialogue with visible spirits, scene changes, and fantasy framing; a mostly empty screen with a dialogue box should be judged low even if the text advances correctly. Score 0 if there is no readable advancing dialogue system, if text appears all at once with no pacing, or if the player cannot control progression.

At each spirit encounter the game presents a real choice among distinct bargains — bind a pact, refuse, or deceive (or equivalent options) — the player selects one, and the story visibly continues down the path that choice implies. Different options should produce different authored beats that can be seen in the resulting dialogue, pact roster, spirit reaction, later encounter, or ending path. Score 0 if there are no choices, if all options lead to identical content, or if selecting an option has no observable effect.

Binding a pact records a persistent piece of state — the pact joins a tracked collection the player carries — and that collection is visible to the player (a list, icons, or roster of bound spirits/pacts) and changes as they bind or break pacts. Refusing leaves them unbound to that spirit. Score 0 if pacts are not tracked, if the player's bound pacts are never shown, or if binding has no recorded state.

The carried pacts gate later content: which options, lines, encounters, or endings are available depends on which pacts the player holds. A later beat must demonstrably depend on an earlier pact decision — a spirit that trusts or refuses the player because of the company they keep, or an ending unlocked only by a particular set of pacts — not just the most recent click. Score 0 if pacts never affect anything beyond a list on screen, if all content is reachable regardless of pacts, or if the story is effectively linear.

The full loop is connected without restarting the executable: title -> the journey begins -> meet spirits and choose pacts through the branching road -> reach an ending -> return to title or set out again to play differently. Every transition is reachable through in-game UI, and reaching an ending halts progression on a styled result screen. Score 0 if any transition is missing or broken, if the journey cannot reach an ending, or if replaying requires relaunching the application.

The road offers several distinct spirits/familiars to encounter, each with its own name, voice, temperament, the power it offers, and the price it asks — a gentle hearth-spirit, a proud beast, a whispering demon, and so on — not reskins of one generic spirit. Different spirits should pose different bargains and tempt the player differently through specific voice, cost, and consequence, not interchangeable labels. Score 0 if there is only one kind of spirit or a single encounter.

The pacts a player can carry meaningfully diverge play: across the demos at least two different pact-dependent outcomes are observable — an option, scene, line, or ending available to a summoner bound one way but closed to another. A demon-bound summoner and an unbound one should reach materially different content over more than a single swapped sentence. Score 0 if pacts never unlock or block anything, or if all content is reachable regardless of pacts.

The journey reaches multiple distinct endings (crowned among monsters, champion of the unbound, betrayer alone, peacemaker, etc.) meaningfully different in outcome and tied to the pacts and choices the player made. At least two different endings are observable across the demos as authored conclusions reached through different pact paths, not isolated debug screens or the same ending retitled. Score 0 if there is only one possible ending or no real ending.

The world has narrative breadth: several distinct encounters or chapters along the road, recurring or returning characters and factions, and situations that pose different dilemmas, so the journey has varied material rather than one encounter repeated. Extra encounters should have distinct situations, voices, costs, and consequences. Score 0 if there is only a single short scene with no breadth.

The writing delivers an authored summoner's tale with real stakes: spirits and characters have voice, the costs and loyalties of pacts are felt in the prose, and tempting or treacherous bargains can lead to darker outcomes. The content should read as sustained authored prose with scene texture, escalation, and consequences; a few strong isolated lines should not carry an otherwise thin demo. Score 0 if there is no actual narrative content or only lorem-style placeholder text.

Core reading UI is clear and stable at 1280x720: dialogue/narration text is legible in a framed box or panel with an identifiable speaker, choice options are clearly presented and distinguishable, and the player's bound pacts are readable when shown. Nothing critical overlaps or is clipped. Score 0 if dialogue, choices, or the pact roster are unreadable, overlapping, or clipped.

Player input produces clear, immediate feedback: making a pact choice shows a selected/highlighted state and is acknowledged, binding a pact visibly updates the carried-pact display, and advancing a line changes the text, scene, speaker, spirit portrait, pact state, or choice state. Feedback limited to instant text replacement in the same static layout should be judged low. Score 0 if input produces no visible response, or if it is impossible to tell that a pact or choice registered.

Structure is legible: the player can tell where they are — title vs. encounter vs. choice vs. ending — and transitions between encounters and into endings are clear rather than abrupt jumps with no framing. Endings read unmistakably as endings with their own authored presentation. Score 0 if phases are visually indistinguishable or transitions are confusing.

Overall visual style is coherent and authored across title, encounters, choices, and endings: a consistent atmospheric fantasy-journey art direction with a deliberate palette, typography, and framing. The game should look like a published visual novel, not a debug screen of text and buttons. Strong submissions rely on real illustrated or pixel-art treatment; a scene built mainly from ColorRects, circles, silhouettes, and flat primitive shapes should be judged low even if the colors are coherent. Score 0 if the screen is dominated by default Godot widgets, untextured rectangles, or placeholder text.

Backgrounds and spirits use real illustrated or pixel-art assets: encounter scenes have illustrated backdrops and the spirits/familiars are shown with sprites/portraits that establish their nature and mood, not bare colored shapes or empty backgrounds. Different spirits should look visibly different. Bare rectangles, circles as spirits, empty backdrops, or simple geometric stand-ins should receive little credit. Score 0 if scenes have no background art and spirits are absent or are plain rectangles/circles.

The dialogue box, name plates, the bound-pact roster/display, choice menus, title, and ending screens use styled, themed UI — illustrated frames, ornamented panels, custom or styled fonts, pact sigils or icons fitting the fantasy theme — rather than raw default Godot Label/Button/Panel defaults or plain flat boxes. UI polish should make the game feel like a ritual pact interface, not just readable text containers. Score 0 if primary UI is obviously unstyled default widgets.

Presentation has motion and polish appropriate to a visual novel: text reveals progressively, binding a pact animates (a sigil lighting, an icon added with a flourish), scene/spirit transitions use fades or slides, and there is atmosphere rather than instant hard cuts everywhere. Motion should support the mood beyond text appearing in a box. Score 0 if nothing on screen ever animates and every change is an instant snap.
